analysis.name: Alexandra Nichifor
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 209 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Fun professor with clear lectures. Super accessible for help and office hours. Only downside is class difficulty didn't match the final: lecture and homework felt much easier than what showed up on the final, so it was tough to earn a strong grade. I'd recommend her, but find harder practice problems early so the final doesn't blindside you.

Her midterms weren't overly difficult, but since it's Calc 2, you either understand the material or you don't. The department final was noticeably harder than the midterms, which felt like a big jump. Overall she was a solid teacher, though there's still some room for improvement, but I would recommend taking her over other teachers.

One of the best professors I've ever had. Prof. Nichifor really cares about her students and wants them to do well, and is available and willing to meet with you to make that happen. Her tests were hard but very fair; wouldn't test on anything we didn't explicitly go over. Do whatever you can to get into her class, she is amazing!

She's a really good professor who truly loves to help her students out. She was willing to stay in the hall even after the lecture ended to answer questions or to clarify concepts for me. Her structure for the class was clear, and her lecture was also fantastic and easy to understand.  She also gave out notes and outlines for the class. LOVE HER!

LOVE NICHIFOR! Her accent is lovely, and she really cares about her students. She is clear about everything, screencasts, and her tests are certainly not torture. She is clear about what things you should know and what you will be tested on.

analysis.name: Usama al-Binni

analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 106 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: This dude doesn't have a good structure for teaching. His lectures are scattered, homework is a pain, plus there's pre-class quizzes that he posts only a day in advance. I spent most of my time reading the book. I worked through whatever practice midterm he posted. But guess what? The tests were totally different in question type and difficulty

When he talks its like his words have no substance, it literally does not make sense. You really have to spend time studying a lot by yourself for certain topics for them to make sense. Theres a lot of homework and the textbook is also useless. His practice midterms are NOTHING like the actual test so prepared to be screwed over.
Avoid if possible... the worst... he's a new professor and UW made a mistake hiring him.

He talks in a monotone, says um and ah way too much, doesn't teach anything and reads off his slides that are basically copied from the textbook. His practice exams are full of errors and mistakes and he's overall a very frustrating professor.

Too smart. Doesn't bother to actually teach the information. He just puts it on a slide, reads the information, and makes you figure out how to solve stuff when practicing for an exam.  And then when he shows you how to solve practice problems before an exam. He solves w/o showing work and using methods he doesn't teach you.

analysis.name: Mandy Schivell
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 136 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: BIOL 240 Intensive Introductory Biology: Human Health Emphasis It is 15 credits and all of intro biology in ONE 10 WEEK SUMMER QUARTER! It is not easy but doable. Schivell was one of three profs. Her lectures were clear and test questions were ok, she needs to work on making test questions clear about what she is asking which she self admits.

While Mandy does come to class excited and tries her best to be a good lecturer, I personally found her slides a bit disorganized. And when she is teaching, it tends to get very messy with her drawings and descriptions. However, I believe this is just a matter of personal preference, as everyone else I know has liked her a lot. She was consistent.

Releases exams beforehand with redacted values to reduce test anxiety. If you work to understand the content these are basically godsent and will get you the grade you want. You can drop 1 of four exams including the final, so I got to prioritize my other finals. Challenging content, but fairly good explanations and very sweet person overall.

Dr. Mandy does a great job explaining all of the content, however, her slides are very disorganized and she often doesn't post the annotated versions of her slides after class. Labs are very helpful to reinforce content and the TAs are great! The public exams were the best part about this class, they help teach you how to study honestly

Kindest, sweetest professor I have had at UW. She publishes her exams before you take them, removing test anxiety. The only things I don't like is that she uses POGILS, they waste class time and are very unhelpful, and that her lecture slides are hard to study from because they're basically incoherent. otherwise good!

analysis.name: Alexander Mamishev

review_highlights.comment: I genuinely think the positive ratings on here are a PSYOP. Went to lecture for 5 weeks, just listened to him complain about his lazy TA's that wouldn't fix all the problems he created. In terms of the work, it is incredibly easy once you understand his terrible class design. Take it with friends so you can work together in the group projects.

This course is essentially the professor stroking his own ego with irrelevant anecdotes and hyping up chatgpt. For a technical writing course, there is zero critical thinking as AI is both encouraged and worshipped. Extremely confusing google sheets system for course organization and subjective “XP points” that count for 24% of the grade.

This is the most disorganized and confusing professor I have ever had. None of his "lectures" were structured and were mostly either irrelevant rambling or taught by TA's. He claims to want feedback but dismisses real objective criticism. His grading system is absolutely ridiculous and assignments in his class are objectively just busywork.

Although I couldn't understand anything the professor said in class, but he added a touch of fun to my otherwise boring life. I always wanted to laugh whenever he spends half the lecture argue with his TAs. I can't imagine what he would look like in person walking into the IEB. He speaks like an ancient god and his headphones are his true identity.

This class often left me speechless, laughing in disbelief, and learning more from nyt games than anything the professor ever taught us. I have never met anyone who worships chatGPT more than this man which would have been funny if he weren't a communications professor. I called this class Rage Bait 101, and it lived up to that name all quarter.

analysis.name: Ali Karimirad
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style

review_highlights.comment: He's an amazing professor! He provided free textbook excerpts for the material covered, which made the class more accessible. His teaching style and resources show how much he cares about students' success. He helped me regain confidence in economics. Stay on top of assignments, watch the pre-lecture videos, and use the resources to do well! 😊

Not a great lecturer, hard to understand, and tends to go through his slides far too quickly. The problem sets take forever, and many of his questions tend to be worded strangely. TA was great and taught me far more than the professor. Only posts 1 practice exam per test, watch the pre-lecture recordings for the key concepts. Tests are pretty hard.

first module of the class was very easy, second and third modules were quick-paced and much more difficult. his lectures were confusing, but the textbooks helped. class was not the worst if you made sure to keep up, but exams can be harsh as they're not worth many points. did a good amount of self-studying because my TA was not super helpful

Nice guy, taught all the material well. Participation not required, but recommended as it counts for 5% of your grade (polleverywhere). 3 main non-cumulative tests that are pretty hard and only have one practice test each. I didnt read the textbook, not necessary. I recommend going to lecture, quiz, and watching the videos for a good grade.

Ali was a great professor for this class, especially because he was good at explaining the content in detail without brushing over important parts. Attendance was mandatory through poll everywhere, but there were some you could get dropped. The tests were difficult for some people, but I found that if I studied ahead of time they weren't that bad.

She's learning! Some test questions were a little out there. You could tell she was nervous and figuring out how to teach. She is super caring and will prob be really good in a few years.

analysis.name: Andrew Loveless
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 524 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Loveless was perhaps one of the best teachers I've had in all my years of schooling. His weekly emails with homework hints and supplemental readings showed how much he cared about his students' success. His amiable personality and dad-humor lightened the mood, while his lectures were very clear and never tangential. So much gratitude for Andy.
If you do a lot of practice tests before exam, it would help a lot
Awesome professor with absolutely tons of extra resources that he makes himself and makes freely available. Never met someone who was so passionate about math. People do well on his exams.
He gives so much extra help and is super caring. Tests are easy enough that people mostly lose points for sign errors and silly stuff, which makes the curve a bit vicious.

I think the only reason he doesn't have a 4.0 rating is because students who didn't put in effort were upset at their grade. AMAZING professor, the most genuinely kind professor you'll probably ever meet, and teaches very effectively(he makes concepts easy to learn/understand). He wants you to succeed and makes it very easy to do so.

analysis.name: Anthony Gill
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Gill gave a top-notch class, always with a new surprise around the corner. Lectures were entertaining and informative, and Prof. Gill challenged us to think critically and thoroughly. Lots of reading, but the books are pretty good, particularly Leeson's "The Invisible Hook". Prof. Gill makes you work hard, but it's 100% worth it.

A libertarian, and makes it clear via course material. He is pompous and rude, as others have stated. 
I was on good terms with him and received an A in the class, but that does not blind me to the fact that he openly embarrassed students like during the final, where he scolded someone for cheating on the essay loud enough so that we all heard.

Condescending boomer who somehow manages to make simple concepts difficult to understand and doesn't seem to care much about the success of his students. Essentially told everyone that our votes “don't matter” without providing much nuance at all during election season. You would be best served taking other Poli Sci courses.

I had him for the EFS class, and he was great.  Its a lot of reading but the tests are straighforward- there are no surprises.  Not an easy class, but I wouldn't call it hard.  He's nice, funny, and understanding.  He is so interested in what he teaches and that makes him a great prof.  Just get ready to read, but his class is interesting.

Funny professor with quite interesting lectures. Unfortunately the amount of books he requires in this class is just not reasonable at all. Never had a course in my 3 years at UW require this much, easily $100+. And most of the books are written by colleagues of his, so theres no doubt in my mind that they were chosen on purpose. Not for everyone.

If I'm a sophomore or junior taking his 337 class I'd just quit the major honestly, his class was so boring and the fact that he was forced to teach this make me so sad. Most boring class I have ever take in UW. The course content is so important that the lectures shouldn't be presented by professor like this. He's just look at a single slides talk about whatever he thought about, and test it in the quiz

One of the harshest grade scales I've seen. Professor Banerjee had no desire to teach this class and it showed. analysis.name: Daniel Bessner
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 19 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: His lectures are very clear, they do tend to have a lot of content at a fast pace, but he always encourages questions from students. Homework is super managable only brief readings each week, with weekly quizzes on lecture/reading content. Professor really cares for each student. One of the most intelligent professors I've had at UW. I RECOMMEND!!

Super fast and dense lectures with almost no notes on his slides. I honestly think he writes his lectures beforehand and reads them like a script. That said, he's funny enough that I genuinely didn't care about that. He's also willing to answer any questions that you might have. Exams are pretty easy too.

Very, very intelligent and accessible.  The class was lecture-heavy, but asking questions was heavily encouraged.  Graded on very few meaty assignments, but prep was easy to do.  Cares a lot about learning and current affairs, definitely a class to take if you can.

Bessner is such an amazing professor. His class was my favorite class at UW. The class was discussion based, and every discussion was incredibly intellectually engaging without being too difficult to understand. His lectures were also super interesting, and I really appreciated hearing his perspective on a lot of current events. Take his class!!

Easy grader, but he talks really fast, doesn't use slides, and if you don't take notes you will struggle. Make note of overarching concepts and events but don't worry about the details of the events, just note what caused them and their overall impact. Do the readings and take notes on them, referencing them boosts your exam grade.

analysis.name: Dean Bretland

Soft-spoken but gives you the ability to excel in the class if you aren't afraid to ask questions. His lectures are fantastic, and his wording and teaching style makes everything very easy to understand. Hard class for sure, but it could have been a lot worse without him teaching it. Never makes you feel ashamed of asking questions. Lots of practice material is given, curve was not very good at all though.

Worst professor I've had. I need to like visually see explanations and how to work through stuff. He reads from the slides and only the slides, never did his own work and never actually explained the correct answers, only what was on the slide. I stopped going to lecture because it was pointless. Will be retaking. Don't waste out of state tuition

real chill guy. stuff you learn and practice in lecture is very relevant to exam content. problem explanations were a bit lacking however, and a lot of conceptual learning comes from the textbook since lecture is mostly spent working on problems. overall a very approachable professor and seems like he really wants you to succeed

overall a likable and fun guy- could be a bit better at making lecture more concise but honestly I didn't mind that much
did some cool work with archaeological analysis of population numbers which was never mentioned in class but its like wow this guy is teaching stats I wish I could know more about how he used it and whatnot

Very nice guy, good content and lecture. Hard concepts but the assignments were easy, so not too hard to get an A. Was a bit disorganized / late to post things on canvas tho. A great intro class to coding on R.

Concepts were easy but his lectures were extremely dry and monotone. Sections were useful sometimes, but section activities are short and inconvenient to get to as a commuter. He's a nice guy and you can tell he wants his students to learn. His lectures are easy to follow. I took Sociolinguistics, an incredible course for those interested in linguistics. She is a tough grader and there are research assignments throughout the quarter. If interested in studying under her, I recommend speaking to her about what she expects on exams because she is particular. Go to class, it'll help. Take if serious about linguistics.

rubrics and assignment guidelines all incredibly unclear. clearly passionate, but lectures were poorly paced and often boring. the only reason i didn't drop this class was because i needed the credit, and it wasn't even a hard class. despised her teaching style and every single assignment made me want to bend my laptop in half.

Quiz sections are optional aside from the midterm. Prof Evans does really care about the topic but the lectures do get boring at times. She is very nice though and quite understanding. Pretty easy class, I did not read the textbook once but I do recommend going to lectures if you don't read the textbook.

Passionate about her subject and getting the class to participate. Weekly assignments/quizzes are easy and not timed. However, the midterm was bad. Idk if that was the TA's grading, but somehow I managed to get a 74 while getting 90-100 on everything else including a 95% on the final I didn't study for. Took down my grade to a 3.6 (91).

Straight up worst teacher i have ever had. His english is poor, but thats not why he is unclear. He just spends way too much time on theory and does way too few example problems. There is 0 reason to go to lecture, if anything it makes things more confusing. I would suggest using the time you should be in class to go over his lecture powerpoints.

A very mediocre professor. He seems to be really intelligent and knowledgeable, but when it comes to actually teaching, he lacks clarity, and his classes are really boring. analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Coursework is manageable however grading was unclear and most assignments had little to no feedback. Instruction and expectations extremely vague especially with the final. Class participation is essential every class.

The class was super easy and requirements were clear. But she had multiple non emergency class cancelations and didn't seem to care at all about the class. Small group discussions were nice but the way she responded back to peoples opinions seemed very passive aggressive and blase.
Davinder is a pretty good teacher, but the class was extremely boring. As long as you do the readings, the class is cake. Only 2 tests, with study guides given. However, it is a REALLY boring class.

Professor Bhowmik runs a very solid discussion-based class. JAPAN 435 leaned heavily on theoretical texts, which were harder to discuss than literature, but the central ideas and themes were very interesting. I also enjoyed seeing her research interests incorporated into the class, as well as her openness to learning from us as well as vice versa.

Do the readings and participate as much as possible and you'll do fine. Something I really love about Prof. Bhowmik is that she's not biased and actually likes when people play the devil's advocate. She's also very accommodating if you need to take your final early for studying abroad which was awesome.

Attendance isn't "mandatory" but strongly encouraged, although she is flexible with dates on assignments and gives lots of feedback on assignments and essays. Not a lot of work weekly other than reading.

Fairly easy A, and she is passionate. But... she forces her opinions down others throats. She's nice to me, but was very degrading to many other students.

The classes do revolve on participation and active learning.

analysis.name: Katherine Beckett
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 57 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: AMAZING PROFESSOR. My favorite who made me de ide to major in LSJ. A difficult class with lots of reading, but WORTH IT and that is coming from a stoner. Interesting concepts of war on drugs and criminalization of the minority. Super relevant to today and its common knowledge that she is a tough class so don't let that think that affects too much

Best class ive ever taken and amazing professor, her lectures were great and the readings were extremely interesting. 
Two essay-based exams and about 5-6 quizzes, you can take the quizzes 3 times each. In class assignments are easy and given about once a week. Discussions with other students are encouraged in class.
Professor Katherine is amazing!

I LOVE Prof Beckett! LSJ 200 was an amazing class and made me want to major in LSJ. Beckett is well-spoken, articulate, caring, and passionate. She engages students in discussion, which is interesting in a large lecture hall, but I enjoyed it. The class is organized well into 3 major units, with either a test or paper at the end of each. Take it!

Overall, do not take this class if you do not plan on minoring or majoring in LSJ. While Katherine and the TAs are all great, the class content is very repetitive. The reading responses/assignments honestly feel like busywork. All in all, I feel like way too much work is asked for from a 200 class. This class made me stray away from the LSJ dept.

intro to law. class format: 3 essays (2-3 pages each; each worth 10% of final grade), a midterm (very easy, class average was 88%; worth 20% of your final grade) a final (10 page essay; worth 30% of final grade) and sections grade (based on small quizes and participation in discussions; worth 20% of final grade. class focused on immigration law.

analysis.name: Colleen Craig
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge

review_highlights.comment: Honestly I don't understand why people like her so much. Dr. Craig is the definition of a teacher in a weed out class. Don't get me wrong she is a good lecturer but her exams are hard and super tricky. If you make one mistake that answer will appear on the exam as they are multiple choice. She provides practice material that is very unprepatory, because her exams end up being 10x harder. Her TAs are horrid too. I will never take a class with her again.
Makes her test as hard as possible. Gives exam reviews that are so much easier than the actual test. Would never take her again.
If you aren't medicated before taking this class, call your psychiatrist ASAP. Even God can't guide you through this class.

Dr. Craig is a great professor. She's the type of professor that if you have to move your schedule around a bit to get her, it's worth the hassle. 152 is definitely tough, but her lectures reinforce the textbook well, she gives ample study materials for the exams, and you can tell she really cares about the students. Plus, she's pretty funny.

Carly was awesome and helped me push myself creatively. She helped me discover my painting style, and I looked forward to going to class every day. She also provided materials for us to use in class, so we didn't need to buy more materials, which I appreciated.
