Tests are difficult but study hard and you can do well.

I think Novitski was one of the best professors I've had so far. She's super knowledgable about physics and is a really good lecturer, I'm not really good at physics but she makes it super easy to understand. Just watch out for the tests as they are really difficult and the MCQ portion has questions that are similar to FRQs.

Highly underrated professor- most of the complaints against her have more to do with how college courses are run and less with her teaching. Her lectures were super nutritional and the course materials were highly organized and helpful. She was extremely open and receptive to feedback and used to tailor lectures. Don't be afraid to take her course!

the class structure is an absolute mess. furthermore, professor novitski is very unorganized in lecture spends long points talking about simple or irrelevant subjects while glossing over the much more challenging materials. finally, the class simple doesn't prepare you for the exams most people were pretty much clueless the entire quarter.

Not a bad professor, some explanations are better than others. Not enough time is given to work on problems in class, so you'll have to spend plenty of time outside of class. Do as much practice as you can and make sure you can complete the practice tests within the time you'll get for the real thing (some patterns will be repeated).

analysis.name: Eric Reel
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Exercises are manageable, and projects can be easy or time-consuming. Exams are difficult, and he gives little guidance on what to expect. Rubrics are vague, and asking questions usually gets “do what's reasonable.” Overall, if you're comfortable teaching yourself, you'll probably do fine, but if you rely on guidance, this class can be frustrating.

Great and funny guy. Exams are based on implementation rather than theory. His lectures are kind of confusing, so just focus on learning how to code what he talks about. Exercises build up to what you'll do in projects. About 2~3 exercises/projects a week. Very open and good at answering questions. One project late pass. Expect to learn on your own

I took CSS342 and CSS343 with Professor Reel and passed. He uses LeetCode for practice and assigns fun coding projects. The class is more exercise/project-heavy than lecture-heavy, which I enjoyed. Use his office hours and ask questions! He truly cares and even checks if exams were too hard, sometimes allowing flexibility in projects and pacing.

Reel is a great professor, but his teaching style is not super easy. Im rating him for 343 but I also took his 342 and it was the same deal. His exams and projects are based off Pisan's class but I believe his teaching style is better, especially when I cared about the class. For the most part if you focus on class youll learn enough to 3.5+
funny guy, reasonable workload, 1 full page of notes for tests. would take again!

analysis.name: Erik Hurlen
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 44 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Had Hurlen for AA210 Statics and it was great! Watching the lectures online and then classes were solely solving problems. Worked great for getting the information and being able to rewatch everything and then figure it out and understand it better as we worked through problems in class. Labs once a week were also great as another summary. 10/10

Great professor in class, although the classes were mostly just working on problems. Accessible outside class and reasonable. Exams were difficult, with averages at ~60% for most. Grading methods are questionable, as his class 'curve' is extremely ambiguous, He just "looks at a spreadsheet" to determine final grades low avg grade for the class.

Professor Hurlen runs the class very well, with fair tests and generous HW policies. The in class time is entirely working through practice problems, with the expectation you watch the pre lecture videos to learn the content. It's not my favorite way to structure a class, but isn't really bad either.

Good professor, a little condescending. His grading is based upon how he feels, so if you have a problem with it, you have to email him as he has no rubric or guidelines. He cares a lot and will work with you to find success. Dry humor. Very on top of things.

I haven't taken his in person classes but he is a terrible online educator. Most of the examples he just assumes you know a bunch of steps and skips through them, so if you can't attend lecture due to time differences you won't understand it. Even if you do ask he just gives vague answers like "look at the image again" or ignores it completely.

analysis.name: Kathie Friedman
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 17 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: She's a great professor. Her selection of readings will give you a well-rounded understanding of contemporary migration issues. Admittedly it is a lot of reading if you do all of them, but if you're a quick reader it'll be ok. She likes to have group discussions in class so you'll get to know your classmates better than in a regular lecture.

My favorite professor ever. While this class was quite reading dense, they are all interesting to some level, and she fits in a few documentaries that were really fun to watch. Going to lectures is a must, not only because it will benefit you in exams, but because she cares a lot about what she teaches and class time is quite fun.

I adore Professor Friedman!! Her class content was organized, interesting, and important. The books she required were well written and fun to read. Her exams were take home. She is also so kind, knowledgeable, and willing to help! She provides you with outside opportunities if you put in the effort. One of my favorite professors for sure!

Friedman is wonderful. She knows her stuff about the history if Jewish women and is so open to her students' ideas and opinions. It is clear she enjoys teaching and cares about her students. I recommend taking a class with her!
Her class was wonderful to take and she made learning enjoyable. The readings were always helpful, she never gave busy work, and she was always very clear on what she expected out of the class.

analysis.name: Fanny Dos Reis
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 92 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Now I finally understand why her class always have open spots, DON'T take her class, her lecture is useless and she does not care if you understand the content or not, and she is super lazy, you don't get your midterms back until the end of the quarter and she doesn't even give you the answer for the midterm. She also doesn't showup to office hour.

Prof. Dos Reis' lectures somewhat follow the structure of the textbook, but in-class proofs are often different than the text. She does not spoon-feed proofs and instead has the class work together to solve a problem. Homework is difficult but there are not many assignments. Her accent is not hard to understand.
Worst instructor ever. Only few students really listen and understand. Lots of mistakes on the notes.

One of the worst professors I've ever had. Her lectures were messy and all over the place, very unorganized, and her handwriting was extremely difficult to read. She's a great person and she's very nice and flexible with accommodations when needed, but she was still a horrible teacher.

Her lectures for 207 is pretty comprehensive and easy to follow. Apart from her strong french accent that can be hard to understand, she was able to explain the process and concepts well and clarifies people's questions effectively. Sometimes she comes late to the lectures which for a 50 minute lecture it can affect how much we learn by a lot.

analysis.name: Fanze Kong
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Practice Final does not really useful for the actual Final Exam. DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS!

He s very patient. He explains the homework steps clearly and makes them easy to understand. I asked some questions more than once , and he always explained them again patiently   The class pace is just right, which helps me follow along well
The class notes are very clear and well organized. The exam questions are similar to the homework, so as long as you do the homework carefully, it's not hard to get a good grade
Seven homework are very tough and the average Midterm quiz grade for Amath 403/503 is 55%, which is terrible. If there is no Midterm correction, there is no way to pass. Please do not take! DO NOT!
Professor Kong is excellent. His way of explaining concepts is very well suited for mathematics students, and I learned a great deal from his lectures!

analysis.name: Frank Macarthy
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 17 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment:
An angel among mortals. Just take him. 
Barely any homework, always gives extensions, super chill in-class group assignments, lenient grader, accessible outside of class. Three major projects that are interesting and useful in real life. Tells funny stories and swears in class. 
Best professor I've ever had!

Yup, Frank is the real deal and a teacher by heart. Class is engaging, material is interesting, and projects allow you to give your own personal spin while routinely engaging with the perspectives of your classmates. This course absolutely made my quarter, but don't take it if you're not ready to socialize.

One of the best profs I've ever had. Cares abt students & learning, very kind w/ extensions and workload, knows & admits his class wont always be top priority. Welcoming environment, he's very suited to the small class size + does great with it. Incredible prof and person

If you want the easiest A, take this class. There is one project in the class over the quarter that you work on that's super easy. He rarely gives homework, with very little reading for a free write the next day about the readings. Attendance isn't mandatory, but there is a lot of in class assignments that are super easy. Show up and u wil get 100%
The best prof I've had honestly, he actually cares about his students and what he's teaching. Made me feel interested in an English class which is wild

analysis.name: Benjamin Feintzeig
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Great professor; great at explaining complicated topics in a way that inspires you to want to read more in your free time. Reading responses are graded very generously, but the lectures are so interesting that he makes you want to read closely anyway. I learned a lot about philosophy of science in this course and was pushed to think for myself.

His lectures were great, just a little long. He answers questions well and is very helpful during office hours. The class is also difficult, but he does a really good job teaching, so it's not impossible. Also, the final was a take-home, so do with that information what you will.

Excellent lecturer, truly knows his stuff! Homework's and Final were fairly difficult but graded very leniently. Very interesting material presented in an effective way where often you will prove parts of results shown in lecture.

Fantastic professor, class content is diabolically hard but very possible to succeed in. I don't really do STEM which is probably why I struggled, but I still scored highly consistently. Just make sure you turn everything in on time!! Feintzeig is actually the goat though, incredibly intelligent and really easy to work with outside of class.

I have so much to say about Professor Feintzieg. Not only is he incredibly knowledgeable, but is also incredibly accommodating -- his class was difficult, but difficult in the way that you leave the course with a completely changed mind and as much passion as him. I absolutely recommend him for PHIL470 (and honestly for anything else) :)

analysis.name: Megan Francis
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 55 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Incredibly passionate lectures with the knowledge to back. Taken 3 classes of hers now, and I will say that the upper courses are heavy on readings. They're engaging readings though, so if you try your best, you can easily engage in discussions effectively. Also accessible with her office hours with wonderful insight on class and career prospects:)

The lectures were amazing and very interesting to listen to. However a downside is that Prof. Francis doesn't post her slides or record her lectures. If you miss a class you have to get notes from a friend. The lectures were basically just a summary of the reading. The final however had some dumb questions that tanked the grade.

I really enjoyed this class. It was incredibly difficult as someone who doesn't test well when its in essay format. I was also sick for a week and fell behind, but her lectures and enthusiasm was amazing. She's incredibly intelligent and personable. Do the reading and keep up with the material.

VISIT HER OFFICE HOURS! shes very intimidating in person, but during office hours one of the sweetest people ever! Get ready to study, show up to lectures, and take notes because if you dont you will not pass.

Absolutely loved professor Francis! Her lectures were clear and interesting, and the material was relevant and fun to learn. She doesn't post her slides so go to class. Focus on lecture material for exams, read if you need supplementation on a topic. Quiz section was always helpful and interesting too and I loved my TA, Carolyn. 100% recommend.

analysis.name: Tyler Fox
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 48 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I don't know why his credibility as an instructor is often questioned. He taught the class fairly well, and I learned some neat methods. Though I will say he had his moments of being entitled and forthright about his opinions being fact.

I wanted to believe Tyler was different. I tried so hard to convince myself that he actually cared about me and my goals. In the end, he just became every other professo who just sits by having little to no involvement with their students. Normally, I wouldn't be this critical, but he was our studio director, I needed someone to rely on.

He tries to associate with students who he believes hold higher importance and neglects and stone walls students who he perceives having no potential. He relies on external validation to receive social supply to validate his toxic traits and actions.

This class was pretty redundant, and I felt the previous class was good enough. Tyler looked bored and uninterested with the course material and it felt like a slog coming to class. The readings were boring and useless, and grading never made sense. I got marked down for an arbitrary reason for a writing assignment.

My feelings for Tyler are mixed. There were instances where he was compassionate and provided a helping hand when needed. Towards the end, he cared less about my pursuits, and he mostly lounged around the classroom. I can't really say he did much for my growth as a designer, in fact I felt he stunted it.

analysis.name: Farbod Shokrieh
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 72 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: He is overall a good professor. His tone is clear and explains the concepts well. However, he is too old-fashioned, he posts absolutely nothing about the course online, with 0 ai tolerance and no practice exams. EVERYTHING needs to be learned during the lecture. Also an average of 15 pages of homework each week for a math course is diabolical.

I strongly believe most reviews are written by him. I don't know anyone in the course that would call him a great professor. Lectures were all proofs without explaining how to do the math, he made TAs do everything, his syllabus was just reiterating to not contact him, and he did not submit a single grade until the last week of the quarter.

Professor Shokrieh is clearly very smart and knows quite a lot about math, however he evidently did not care about this class at all. He's not very good at making things understandable and spent most of the time just going over proofs that didn't matter much, in addition to being very inaccessible. My TA was quite good though and helped a lot.

I emailed twice regarding DRS accommodations and general grading and his response for both was to ask the TA who could not help. The syllabus basically tells us not to reach out at all.The biggest problem however is that it is currently the last week of class and not a single grade has been uploaded into canvas despite TAs having graded everything.

Lectures are well-structured and easy to follow. Attendance is mandatory as there are no online lecture notes or video recordings. There is a ton of homework (15 questions, each with subparts some weeks ;-;), but mathematical proofs as a subject are rigorous and require work. His tests were extremely reasonable if you went to lecture.

analysis.name: James Felak
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 58 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Overall, a good professor. While this class is challenging, his feedback is really good and useful- he is the type of professor to actually listen and care about his students! My only complaint is he does not post his slides (which are just images anyway) or any outlines- and in lecture he talks super fast, this class made me type at light speed...

Professor Felak is fascinating, hilarious, and never fails to give an engaging lecture. Use the outlines he provides and type your notes unless you want your hand to cramp. Do not skip class. Be aware that there is Roman Catholic perspective and bias to the lectures; try to distinguish what is objective history and what is a defense of Catholicism.

Dr. Felak talks so much, so fast. He grades very difficult, with an unspecified rubric. He has so many expectations out of your papers and in-class essays, without any rubric or guidelines provided. Doesn't post grades until the day they are finalized at the end of the quarter. Thanks for my C...

Loved his lectures--super engaging and enthusiastic. His office hours were a great time for me to ask clarifying questions. Also had interesting stories from his time in Eastern Europe/post-grad education. Frequently approached him after class for questions and appreciated his support. The assignments are relatively chill, exams are the bulk.

Professor Felak was amazing! Fairly challenging, but rewarding nonetheless. His lectures are so entertaining and provide great context to course content. The three books required to read are very interesting and the short papers you have to write on them are graded somewhat hard but receive great constructive feedback. Very friendly professor!

analysis.name: Lawrence Goldman
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 207 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: 238 and 241 two do not line up and so you go do the stupid experiment without knowing what's going on, and all Goldman says to do read his powerpoints to understand. Yeah bc I have time for that sir? You spend 7 hours in lecture/lab and then like 300 hours outside of lab. Worst time of my life

Larry is the man. He cracks bad (good) puns and makes his lectures thoroughly enjoyable. Since the class is somewhat informal he is quick to answer any questions you may have at any given time during lecture, which is impressive.  Group worksheets (not graded) are atrociously difficult, but group quizzes are fair and help immensely. TAKE THIS CLASS

DO NOT TAKE GOLDMAN! With the current grading system we have, all of our tests are out of 5. If you get a question >67% correct, you get the point. If less than 67, you don't. You can get a 65% on the test and receive a 0/5. Uses some weird token system to redeem points. Do not take him. Too complicated, unprofessional, and stressful.

33 points total on all exams combined. Miss more than 2 points, you lose your 4.0. U do get 4 tokens total to redo questions. He has an all or nothing grading where if you get 4 out of 6 parts of a question correct, you get 0 points on it. Keep in mind each test is 7 points (final is 12). If you ace tests, take this class. If not, take Forrest.

Terrible Professor, he is lazy, slides are directly from the book.  He adds nothing to the class you are better off not going to lecture.  Does not care about student learning.  Interrupts exams to ask if people are there.  The safety quizzes are bull, and SDS pages are crap.  Wish there was another option when I came to ochem labs.

analysis.name: Michael Gelb
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 91 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor GELB will leave you flummoxed and in awe. His profound expertise in alcohol dehydrogenase and styrofoam will inspire you to become a piece of styrofoam yourself. He does several demonstrations in class, and almost considered reacting phenylacetone with methylamine, a complex process.  This class is recommended for its depth. Take GELB.

Gelb is the type of teacher that everyone dreads. He may be smart at research and world applications of chemistry, but he should not be a lecturer. His lectures were unorganized and rambling. Not a single lecture passed without him going off on a student for asking "something you should have known. This is so easy for me." Would not recommend.

1. none of the stuff we learned was on the midterms.
2. he doesn't have lecture slides, he scribbles on his ipad
3. he talks about his time in jail SO MUCH, he thinks hes proud of that
4. doesn't wear shoes
5. doesn't show up during tests
6. doesn't provide his own practice midterms
7. class average 30%
8. all the 5.0 ratings are false
please like

He goes on a lot of in-class tangents, which I like but tend to throw people off. His tests are like mind-wrecking versions of a Sudoku. If you see him outside of class he can be very helpful, but it's hard to learn in lecture. He also makes a lot of jokes and picks on students, which I also think is hilarious, but YMMV. Oh there's no homework!

Don't bother taking him. His lectures are unclear, goes off on tangents, talks about non relevant information, and says "anything said in lecture is fair game on the exam". First midterm was week 7, second midterm was week 9, and we had to convince him to put up a topic summary list. He doesn't want to teach, and he shouldn't be teaching.

analysis.name: Gordon Watts
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 23 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Watts is very funny and nice guy, but he is bad at writing tests, they were extremely different from the practice exams and were not related to the focuses of our class and instead focused heavily on random topics that were barley discussed, and often had random conceptual questions. his lectures are fine but not fantastic
Great professor, really patient when having questions. He does all the processes step by step, so it's easy to catch up with his pace.
Very chill professor. He's cracking jokes almost every single class and teaches the material well too.

Honors teacher. I only go to class for his wit and the clicker questions. Hard tests if you don't know what pitfalls to look for. If you've taken physics in high school, don't get cocky or this class will leave you eating dirt.

A lot of people are focusing on how difficult the class was with Watts. They are forgetting that the class is difficult no matter who you take it with. Watts is a solid teacher, and if someone disagrees with some of his work, he always encourages them to speak up, knowing he might have made an error. He's an honest and funny guy, and great teacher.

analysis.name: Jennifer Gaul
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Self-motivation was VITAL for this class.  A lot of assignments were discussed once, and then we were left to figure out the details on our own.  However, the professor was very kind and attentive, and I felt as though she genuinely cared.  Not a particularly difficult class (I ended with over 100%), but there was a lot of self-teaching here.

I took teaching as a profession in class. She is amazing! I had a really difficult quarter this quarter and worked with me to help get the work done. As long as you can show you care about your grade and try, she's a great teacher! Definitely would take again!

This is the most practical education class I've taken yet. The classes are mostly discussion and project based, not lecture based. However, I did think grading was a little intense - there are 80 points to be earned and a 4.0 is 99%, so you literally cannot miss a single point if you want a 4.0. Also, the 2.5 hour classes did seem to drag on.

If you are looking for a chill but educational class this is the one. There is a lot of reading and some of it is hard to understand but you can skim most of it. The projects aren't too hard but there is way to much discussion in class. Its a 2.5 hour class that could be done in 1 or 1.5 hours.
She's super sweet and you can tell she wants her students to do well. Only complaint is that showing up to class sometimes felt like a waste of time.

analysis.name: Matt Golub
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: A super difficult course. I think Prof. Golub is nice and knowledgeable, but lectures are really hard to follow, HW is long and very underspecified, and the midterm was incredibly hard compared to previous quarters. Response times on Ed from the entire course staff were also very slow, making it hard to seek help.

The professor was okay. Was knowledgeable, kind, and tried decently hard to teach us well, but lectures were poor

The class structure overall is poor (it's only lectures, hws, and finals)
lectures are difficult to follow & the 5 homeworks are long and feel like final projects

Class is feasible, but procrastinators like me should be very careful
Knowledgeable but not a good teacher and communicator. Lecturing is very monotone
fumbles during lectures, doesn't seem interested to teach

analysis.name: Kimberlee Gillis-Bridges
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 32 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: I took the honors seminar course. The syllabus and topic were altered between the time of course registration and the first day of class, so student did not know the topic we would be focusing on and would have taken different sections. The professor is very knowledgeable and cares about the subject, but is an extremely slow and volatile grader.

A decent teacher, but grading criteria were very vague and felt very subjective. Additionally, she didn't get grades in until the last minute, so you couldn't get any feedback. It was very hard to read how she felt about topics when students were discussing them with her.

I'm honestly just disappointed with this class. Something that was meant to be interesting and fun turned into a stressful class. Grades came in after the deadline and were harsher than expected. There is also so much material for the class that is difficult to access. If you're just getting credits done or trying to have fun, take something else.

It's almost July and still no grade back from her—just an X on my transcript. She doesn't give any grades during the quarter, so you have no way to improve/get feedback.

As far as the class, I would recommend. It is interesting and I enjoyed all of it. The professor is disorganized and frustrating sometimes, though. She's very nice outside class.

I took ENGL 207 with this professor. She's incredibly thoughtful and very knowledgeable, but I felt at times that, as a low-income student, her class was too expensive for me to fully participate in, as we reviewed comic books and movies (a topic not listed in the course registration).

analysis.name: GU Liangcai
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 27 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Professor Gu is not as unkind as other people are making him out to be, but you can tell he would rather be anywhere else than lecturing a bunch of undergraduates. His lecture slides are made mostly of random pictures in a random order and you are going to spend much of your time trying to decipher them.

Most. Confusing. Lecutres. Ever. Really needed textbook to understand his content. Unforgiving w partial credit. MC test questions were literally common sense but FRQs were IMPOSSIBLE to prepare for. He was just writing whatever came into his head and had nothing to do with the content. Made Holt organize the entire class. Made course out of order

Professor Gu does not speak great English and his lectures are convoluted. You will be heavily dependent on the textbook. Averages on the test were so low they had to consider restructuring the grading system. Although Michael Holt is a great professor Gu makes it very difficult. I dropped the class because there are no test drops. Do not take.

Professor Gu is confusing in lecture and does not make it clear what students are expected to know. The structure of 441 does not match the practice problems or exams and professor Gu does not make it clear what content we should be studying or which details are important in metabolism. Unfortunately he is the only professor for 441 :(

I have a hard time understanding Prof in lectures but as long as you use your resources to your advantage (TAs and YouTube videos), learning the knowledge is do-able. Took Exam 1 and it was fair. Heavy on doing practice problems to succeed and asking TAs for help. Class is NOT an easy A. Personality-wise, he's nice and easy to approach!

analysis.name: Charles Godfrey
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: A very bad lecturer, but he's a decent person. Spends most of the time explaining concepts that are not relevant and will not be covered in the exams. You get a lot of homework, I mean a lot, more than your 5 credit classes, there is no way that you can finish it, the solutions manual will come in handy. Would not recommend taking him.

If you have him for 307, switch our or just drop the class completely. He assigns about 10-40 (yes 40) hours of homework a week, doesn't reply to e-mails, and his midterm was all application problems that would've taken at least 2 hours to finish. Takes forever to return homework. Comes in late. Bad. Do not take with him.

If you find out that you have him for a class, make sure that you switch out ASAP. His lectures are often ahead of the homework (too far ahead) and doesn't explain well. He doesn't show you how to do example problems but rather spends deriving a lot of stuff that isn't needed and unhelpful for the homework (which is super difficult).
Worst Lecturer I've had in UW, I swear.
He does really bad lectures. 43 minutes to explain a trivial problem and teach us new materials in the rest 7 minutes. NEVER EVER THINK TO TAKE HIS CLASSES.

analysis.name: Jon Herron
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 152 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Jon is an amazing professor! He cares so much about student success. He makes you think outside the box and use reasoning and logic to solve complex problems. The tests are tough and are all short answer, but this is one of those classes that you get a lot more out of other than just a grade.

Professor Herron is like a beacon of light in a dark, dark, dark world. If someone asked me to describe Professor Herron, then I would say "no thank you." But, if pressed I would have to say the following. Professor Herron is a passionate, caring, inspirational, man who's work in the teaching field genuinely enlightens individuals. Take his class.

Dr. Herron is one of the most enthusiastic, lively, and helpful instructors in the Biology department. He provides a lot of assignments, weekly online quizzes, a class research project, clickers, practice exams so there a lot of opportunities to do well if you place the effort. Grading is not just weighted on the midterm and final exams.

Class was intro so very content heavy but his lectures were useful. His use of examples were great and he made sure to explain concepts as simplified as possible. Was a little slow and ended up doing most reviews on my own, but his website with all the past exam questions was EXTREMELY useful and got me through. Hosts accessible office hours.

Super nice professor! He has a very clear rubric for all the assignments he gives and he's very funny and engaging. I learned a lot from the class and it wasn't too hard even though I'm not a STEM student. He makes biology easier to understand and is available during his office hours if you have questions.

analysis.name: Hannah Tang
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 66 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I loved taking 344 with her, thought 333 would be the same. IT WAS NOT! Lots of exercises (22 total). No partners on assignment. She and the TA's are not active on Ed as much as students. HW1 was not graded until Week 8. She only gave 4 late days despite students struggling in the class and asking on Ed. Still waiting for grades on major projects.

Ton of exercises which require knowledge of content that hasn't been covered yet. Homeworks are released late and despite many complaints extensions are not given since "an extra day does nothing." EdStem board is inactive and OH queues take well over an hour. Trick questions on PollEv's to catch students not attending. Grading takes 1.5 months.

no grading scale at all. deductions for everything. one simple missed multiple choice? -6 points. grading isn't even fair between students. some get deducted for the same mistakes while others don't. says attendance is purely attempt based but then hides special words that you need to enter on pollev to get attendance to count.

As many students have mentioned, it took her 1.5 months to get our first HW assignment graded. Granted, there were TA strikes going on...for one day...8 weeks into the quarter. 4 large scale homeworks and a total of 18 small scale exercises worth 35% of your grade, where only ONE is dropped because "learning involves learning from mistakes" lol!

Not very accommodating. Didn't return the grades of a single assignment on time. The homeworks came back on average 1.5 months late. Also, every homework was released late, so our professor would tell us we had 2 weeks to do it, but in reality, we really had like a week. When we asked if they could extend the deadline, she always said no.

analysis.name: Jeffrey Heer
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Grading system is weird. You never start with the full points on an assignment and you have to "earn them back" through very clear extra effort on assignments. On the final project you start with 30/35 points (~86%) and have to go out of your way to earn the other 5. Content is interesting otherwise.

Great professor!
Neat schedule, but annoying/overwhelming to see so many not-really-required "required" readings
Tips
- Didn't realize there was a Textbook
- The Notebook is a different, interactive & free book.
- Lots of "required" reading, but if you focus on the assignments & in-class-practices (with some Notebook review) then you can ignore.

Wonderful lectures and course content as a whole are super useful for research, projects, and general data literacy. Assignments' grading is pretty hard and unclear, but you are practically guaranteed above a 3.5 if you put in just a little effort. No exams either!

What a gem. Jeffrey is one of the best profs in the CS department. He is funny, knowledgeable, and very approachable. Clearly knows his subject matter, and one of the best at Data Visualization. Makes the class fun, approachable, and easy for people of all skill levels in the domain. Highly recomemnd you take his class, you won't regret it.

analysis.name: Mahmood Hameed
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 42 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: This class ramps up exponentially in difficulty, but prof. Hameed is so great at teaching, and explaining. Really knows his stuff. Loved his class so much I want to be a TA (never wanted to be a TA ever for a class) for his class in Spring Quarter for EE 215. This dude is funny and double :thumbs_up:

I've had Professor Hameed for 4 different classes and he is a great lecturer. He genuinely cares about his students and I have learned a lot from him. He is also extremely knowledgeable. However his exams are very difficult so be prepared. Despite that I 100% recommend him though as I feel I have gotten an incredible understanding of each subject.

I can only say that professor Hameed is a great LECTURER. He deliberately make the course content hard in which many of the "knowledge" is not needed for homework nor lab. It's good to know some related contents but the way he presented made it hard to grasp the gist. The pace and the rules for the class were kinda rigid and the TAs were not nice.

Hameed is an amazing professor and a great guy, and it's clear that he's truly passionate about what he teaches. He emphasizes student engagement and provides ample resources to be successful should you put forth the effort. That being said, the homework and exams were very difficult, but the class had a generous curve. Overall very good experience

Hameed is the GOAT! If you get a chance, take any of his classes. He really cares about his students and is passionate about teaching. A great teacher and an even better person. Tests/hw can be hard but only because he wants us to learn. Usually curves the average to around a 3.2. Highly recommend attending office hours.

analysis.name: Matthew Hitchman
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 36 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor Hitchman was one of my first teacher's at UW and he is a fantastic one at that. He very clearly cared about all of his students and was truly such a warm-hearted man. He makes sure that his content stays engaging and works to get everyone to share their thoughts and opinions! If you see his name, register for the class FAST.

Professor Hitchman clearly cares a lot about his students. He is extremely enthusiastic about what he teaches and you can tell he has a genuine passion for literature. His energy made coming to class a delight and he was extremely approachable for help. There was a decent amount of reading, but it is not overwhelming. I heavily recommend!

This class is the best English class I've taken. Class lectures are discussions where he outlines certain points that are very useful in helping complete the assignments. It is a lot of reading, however, I enjoyed all of it, and they were on relevant topics. He is an amazing professor who wants his students to succeed!

Took two classes from Dr. Matthew. Absolutely best English teacher I ever meet. Canvas and courses plan are so clear and he will pretty kind for answer all questions. Giving lots of patience on grading and assignments.
Super nice and caring professor.

analysis.name: Hedwige Meyer
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 32 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I cannot put into words how wonderful Meyer is. She makes the classroom a comfortable environment, and it is clear that she cares about her students. She is from France, so she knows her stuff. She has inspired me to continue with French, partly for my love of the subject and partly so that I can continue under her tutelage. HIGHLY RECOMMEND.

She is hands down the best professor at UW, no competition. She is SO incredibly kind and thoughtful, and one of the rare professors who is genuinely and completely invested in the success of her students. The environment in her classes is so inviting and fun. If you can take a French class with her, you absolutely should, but you will get hooked!

Her classes were wonderful and fun though you really need to participate to get the most out of it. If you have a problem understanding something, she would be there to help you with it. So nice and friendly that it hurts. Definitely would take all of her classes if possible. Just take her classes. Do it.

I ABSOLUTELY ADORE HER! SHE IS BY FAR, A TRUE HIDDEN GEM IN THE VAST SEA OF PROFESSORS AT THE UW. SHE IS SO HAPPY, HER SMILE IS VERY CONTAGIOUS AND JUST MAKES YOU WANT TO SMILE TOO! PLEASE IF YOUR TAKING FRENCH, TAKE HER! DONT EVEN HESITATE!

She's great, and very good at explaining things. It's clear she wants students to succeed. I will say do not buy the textbook - she always has pictures up in class and I don't think I've used my own a single time.

analysis.name: Michael Holt
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 21 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: This man SERVES at 8:30am thrice a week. After every lecture he gains atleast 6*10^23 aura points. If you have holt for biochem consider yourself blessed. God plays favorites and you are one of them. I am surprised bro does not have a backache from carrying the entire biochem dept on his shoulders.

Michael Holt is a really nice professor. He is the most student-careful student that I've ever met. He used a lot of his break time, even Saturday night 8-9 PM, to hold extra office hours to help students to recover their knowledge back. 'm so glad that final exam is mostly made up by his context rather than Gu's, and his question is reasonable.

Prof Holt is an amazing person and professor, so many office hours offered + detailed explanations to questions. I found his test questions more convoluted than Gu's, but they were overall fair. Some lectures were a bit unclear but still leagues better than Gu. Course was out of order (Gu's doing), and Holt often fell behind schedule

This man got me into med school. The single best, kindest, most thoughtful and outgoing professor in the entire UW biochemistry program, maybe one of the best at the whole university. Wrote one of my letters of recommendation, took the time to get to know me, the guy is faultless. Every other professor at UW biochem should aspire to be Prof. Holt.

One of the best professors I've ever met, carrying out everything perfectly. This man is willing to hold professor zoom office hour Saturday night 8pm-930pm just for exam preperation and answering questions! Absolutely wonderful professor, explains everything clearly. Exam difficulty is reasonable, I'd love to have his class again!

analysis.name: Mycah Harrold
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 17 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Professor is barely able to answer students' questions ans students in the class are weirdly motivated to show off thier knowlege in every class wasting roughly 30 minutes of class time. You get nothing out of it but bad grade since grade heavily relies on this class heavily relies on unfair participations.

Proffesor Harrold uses a participation scale that doesn't promote inclusivity. Students are constantly adding information that isn't useful to class discussions. Specifically, there is this girl that waste 30 minutes of class time talking.

She's a nice person and all but she really lacks credibility and prior business knowledge. She constantly wasn't able to answer class questions that would sound quite routine for a professor at this caliber of a school. Participation is unfairly weighted high, favoring those who aren't afraid to speak up at literally anything, for no reason at all.

Professor prioritizes class involvement, graded competitively, encouraging 40+ students to fight for participation. It derails class, wastes time, and prevents learning from the professor they are paying high fees to listen to. Teaching style is fine, but overall engagement is inequitable. Overall attitude and compatibility with students is low.

This professor has unclear and unfair guidelines for writing assignments. If you don't write like Edgar Allen Poe, don't expect to get above an 80% on any writing assignments. Students are pitted amongest each other due to her strict participation scale.

analysis.name: Hunter Little
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: If you have the option to take a class with Hunter, you should 100% take it. You can tell that she truly cares about the material and about everyone in the class. The workload can get heavy at times because still an English class, but it's worth the work. One of the most genuine teachers I've had ever and a useful class as the cherry on top.

Dr Little is a great professor. She cares about her students and how well they are able to keep up with the course. The main project of the class was a group project that was managed very well, but there was also plenty of individual work as well. She is also very forgiving about any absences/late work.

Hunter is an amazing professor who truly cares about the growth of her students. The discussions and readings are engaging and open a whole new world of rhetoric, community, and more to the realm of scientific writing. Hunter makes ENGL199 even more interesting than it already is through her amazing teaching style and communication to her students.

Amazing english instructor. Is very clearly passionate about teaching and is receptive to student feedback and opinion. Class discussions were eye-opening and a great way to form bonds with other students. Overall, I would feel excited and relieved if Dr. Little turned out to be your instructor for any class!
Hunter is amazing! Lot's of work and reading but you learn useful skills

analysis.name: Giovanni Inchiostro
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 21 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: For his first quarter teaching, he wasn't too bad. I will say that he expects you to know a decent amount of algebra in his lectures, especially when it comes to simplification or general algebraic manipulation, and that he sometimes doesn't finish his lectures in time. He is very kind though and can give great feedback and help during office hours

His lectures, at least online, were nowhere near sufficient for the exams. He taught the most basic versions of the concepts and with the difficulty of the homework ramping up dramatically with only two days to do each one, so unless you hate sleep or free time be prepared to never put the textbook down if you want to do well. He means well though.

His lectures are very helpful, and he is always willing to help after class or during office hours. He frequently works problems on the board and walks through them step by step, often engaging directly with the class if students don't understand or need help. There is a lot of homework, but as long as you manage your time, you'll be fine.

He was not a bad professor, very interactive during class, you can tell he cares. At times his explanations were confusing, and you just had to know stuff. I came into calc 1 with no prior knowledge of pre calc, and did alright. Homework was most difficult, a lot of it and it being due at 6:30 PM. He's kind of funny.

For starters, Giovanni is a good guy. He wants to help students understand the concepts. However, his explanations at time can be confusing, and often times, leaves students feeling a bit lost. Moreover, there is lack of practice material that relate to the lectures, aside from the homework.

analysis.name: Ian Schnee
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 77 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: This is the worst class at UW. Phil 120 is not an intro to logic course, but rather a 10-week accelerated study into every minor facet of formal logic. I believe that for this to truly be an intro class, it would have to cut out content and become a series. Schnee wasn't helpful when students were clearly lost, instead just belittled them as "lazy"

I was not expecting this course to be so difficult. Although this is a "intro level" course, the material was extremely advanced. Each week the material becomes more complex, I wasn't able to understand after week 4. Prof Schnee adds questions to exams aimed to trip you up or about content we haven't learned. Avoid this course if you don't need it.
It was an absolute pleasure going to lectures! Always challenges his students and expects a lot but is also ready to help and is open for all questions.

Ian was a great professor- it was clear he loves what he does & wanted us to learn every lecture. Go to office hours- he's super accessible and wants to help you learn. You use the textbook he wrote himself which was clear & easy to follow. Assignments & tests were all reasonable as long as you put in your own effort. Highly recommend his class!

I have never seen a man so excited to teach every day. Probably the most enthusiastic professor I've had so far, and overall just teaches the subject really well. In all honesty, logic was a bit hard for me to follow sometimes, but Ian encourages students to try and be wrong which really helped. Just a fun guy honestly

analysis.name: Ileana Rodriguez-Silva
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 31 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Very energetic and passionate about the subject. Her voice, however, literally hurts my ears as she screams while she lectures. Demands participation in classes of 60+ individuals. She is a very kind person and will help you outside of class if you wish. Lectures are unorganized, very difficult to understand them.

There's way too much homework and readings for a 200 level course. Ive taken multiple 400-500 level courses that had significantly less assignments. I was incredibly stressed the entirety of the quarter by the multitude of readings and content I was expected to read or view in a relatively short amount of time.

Very passionate, kind, and understanding. She definitely cares about her job and her students. I struggled with my mental health that quarter but I talked with her and she helped me come up with a plan, and she checked in a few times to make sure I was doing okay and congratulate me when I managed to turn everything in. Wonderful person in general

I enjoyed her lectures a lot and her class is very interesting. However, we need to read a lot and most of our grades come from the reading reflections. It's important that you write those reflections well. If you pay attention in class and take note, it is not a very hard class.

Ileana is wonderfully enthusiastic and knowledgeable; she's capable of maintaining a classes interest for two and a half hours. however, as others have said, her lectures are unstructured; although I took copious notes, I found the midterm and final very difficult to study for.

analysis.name: Isabella Novik
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 26 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Excellent lecturer. Very easy-to-read lecture slide decks to review. No textbook. Topics covered aren't super challenging conceptually, but you need to know all of the theorems very well for exams. Exams have a high median, usually 80-90%, so there isn't a generous curve. Homework sets can be long (maybe 7-10 proofs) but are otherwise reasonable.

Fantastic professor, first time in a while that I felt going to lectures was worthwhile. Proofs are interactive, she uses great examples and illustrations, and explanations are incredibly helpful. Lecture notes aren't provided before classes, which is a shame, but they are still helpful regardless. Overall one of the best math professors at the UW.

Prof. Novik is the best math professor I've had at UW. She's excellent at explaining quite an abstract subject and clearly enjoys doing so. Further, she's very accessible outside of class for help if you need it, and is just as welcoming and helpful in office hours. I would take her class again in a heartbeat.

This class was hands down the hardest course I took in my undergraduate degree in Mathematics here at UW, however she is a very nice lady and helped me out a lot. Go to office hours the minute you start having problems in the class because she needs to know. Also try to find other people you can study with because it will be a nightmare going solo.
Prof. Novik is one of the best professors I had at UW. Extremely quick to respond to emails, great at explaining  concepts and is very friendly during office hours.

analysis.name: Charles Ives
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 60 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Charles is like an innocent little child. Has a deep affection for Platonic thought. Quirky, yet hilarious. He is quite charming, an unspoiled gem among a lackluster and uninspired Seattle Freeze. Willing to be flexible to see his students succeed. Gets excited about the smallest things. Yes, quirky, but also has a lion's heart. Highly Recommend!

The readings are kind of long but the material itself is easy to grasp and therefore going to class to discuss the reading may seem repetitive. However, the class is what you make of it, so engage in the discussion and you won't be bored! I was never stressed or overwhelmed and he is honestly easy going, friendly, and fun.

First of all, he is simply not an instructor. He does not have anything planned, ever. He babbles about how much of a "different" approach he has on teaching, and never says anything relevant. Half of the class sat in the back and did homework for another class. I barely went to class, should have, but didn't since he led such awful conversations.

Charles' teaching style motivated me to get out the door every day for this class. He's dynamic in his teaching and he exudes genuine joy doing his job. He offers a unique perspective to teaching that other philosophy professors at UW simply don't have. His classes are experiments, so be open to defining expectations as the quarter progresses.

You can tell Charles cares a lot about what he teaches, and how it impacts his students. The course itself consists of readings at home and a short summary (200 words) before the lecture. The lecture was a full class discussion about the readings, based on opinions and the exchange of ideas. He is a great guy and showing up to class was beneficial.

analysis.name: Jonathan Isaac
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I really enjoyed Professor Isaac's class this quarter. He is kind, He takes the time to provide helpful and insightful feedback on homework. I did not have any issues trying to meet for office hours or have to wait a long period for an email response. He is genuinely enthusiastic and cares about the students doing well.

I took ENGL 298. Workload was extremely light, no midterms and no final. Only one group project towards the end of the quarter. Attending class is mandatory and so is participation/ engagement. Extra credit was available. Professor Isaac is extremely sweet and understanding. I would retake this class in a heartbeat.

He is an incredible professor who is very caring and responsive. He thoughtfully addresses everyone's questions and brings in many interesting readings for class, making it possible to learn a lot. He invites guest speakers to share their insights. Truly, he is an outstanding professor, and his course is fascinating. Also, there aren't many works.

Best English class ever taken here. Dr. Isaac is just so cool and chill. Such easy credits since his feedback is the best and his grading is not bad at all. One thing is that this class is mainly focused on participation so be ready to talk a lot every day.

analysis.name: Mack Ikeru
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: It's kinda hard to rate this one because I took his EDUC 240, but the class wasn't his. He was teaching some else's class. So he didn't make any of the assignments or anything. He is very understanding and engaging. Also only 1 class a week, but it was canceled half the time, so no one showed up. Also too much class discussion, not enough lecture

I went to a total of about 3 classes and still passed with flying colors. The content is actually very interesting for an intro but you basically learn nothing new. No exams, no essays, just a portfolio to submit with very lax grading. Not a bad teacher, just boring. Do not recommend if you want actually want to learn about education (ironically).

You can't learn anything from this class. Lectures once a week and he usually cancels the class. He just read the PPT and sometimes feels like he is not that familiar with what he is teaching. Weekly assignments are required but always been asked to write about the same thing/topics. I don't know what they are doing.

Professor Ikeru is a nice guy and very personable. Homework is very, very lax in this class, and Ikeru's lectures are not...super necessary to attend; you can do basically everything out of class. There are some cool guest lecturers though! This class is very much an intro and doesn't present a ton of novel information.

Very kind and caring and funny, although in person lectures felt very unnecessary. Class used ungrading, so we chose our grades at the end of the quarter based on how we felt we did in class, so a 4.0 is relatively easy. This class does have a volunteer requirement, so depending on the place you volunteer it will be a very different experience.

analysis.name: Erkan Istanbulluoglu
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Ist. really struggles with communication, IMO. I often get the sense he hasn't looked at lecture slides since he gave the lecture a year before. He once spent 50min of lecture on rain gauge under-catch. Wording on HW is ambiguous and confusing. He seems personally like a great guy, and probably a great researcher, but not a great teacher.

He's a very kind professor who is accessible outside of class a couple times a week. However, he is pretty disorganized, and his lectures can be hard to follow/understand. Definitely find the textbook somewhere.
Don't take this professor unless it's needed
Very unorganized, lectures are unclear

Just didn't seem to know what he was talking about. Needed correcting many times throughout the quarter, and never really seemed sure of himself. Class was pretty easy, but just seemed like a waste of time. I got a 3.2 only because I forgot to do a couple homework assignments.

analysis.name: Inma Raneda-Cuartero
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 37 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Inma is possibly the most adorable Spanish teacher in the history of existence. Her sense of humor is wonderful if you're paying attention. She has her own course packets which are much cheaper than normal textbooks and online codes. She's a native speaker, and knows what she's talking about, and will help you to go from Spanglish to Spanish.

Inma is SO sweet, funny, all around nice person. She is also super helpful, IF you take the time to contact her. She has helped me so much outside of class, so don't be afraid to approach her. Killer grader on the grammar, so be sure to edit your papers! Would love to have her again
One of the most genuine teachers I've ever had. She knows what she is doing and gives the right amount of work to be able to improve your Spanish without killing you.

I love Inma! If you get the chance to take a class with her do it :) She is amazingly sweet as a person and she doesn't make class hard but you do have to participate and you learn so much. My Spanish improved three fold with her and now I know I have a professor I can talk to. She takes attendance and gets to know your name which is rare at UW.
Inma is such a sweet profesor and cares a lot about her student's learning. If you pay attention and participate in class, you'll do great and genuinely learn a lot.

analysis.name: Erica Ingalls
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 23 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Was not confident about what she was teaching. Does not engage students or have them engage with each other. Lectures are confusing and clearly based directly off the textbook, does not provide examples that are not already listed in the textbook (doesn't seem to understand the material very well). Not open to feedback. No one in class is engaged.

It often feels like she doesn't fully understand the material before teaching. She frequently makes mistakes on worksheets without realizing, which is misleading. I don't understand why we have to write so much in a chemistry class, drawing structures is fine, but she has us writing nonstop, which makes it hard to focus on the actual content.

She was extremely unorganized, every lecture had 40+ handwritten slides with scribbles and arrows that made it hard to follow. She mostly reads directly from the textbook with no additional explanation, and the lecture feels redundant. Always asking if we prefer 220 or 221, I would 100% choose 220. This was the worse course in this equence.

She's very kind, but her teaching style is extremely hard to follow. The lectures are disorganized, and she handwrites everything on the slides, which makes it hard to focus what she talked in lectures. Her handwriting is also difficult to read. You're basically left to teach yourself. If you need structure, this class will be frustrating.

Literally never take her class she just writes and writes and expects u to know what shes talking about. Throughout the lectures its only the same 2 grown people who would answer her questions and people keep not showing up to her lecture bc she doesnt really teach. 0/5

analysis.name: Jodi Newman
analysis.verdict: Excellent choice — highly rated and manageable workload. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 52 reviews — fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Wow. Best professor I had at UW. Her classes are designed with a full lecture with group discussions in every class meeting. Very predictable and consistent. Her lectures are long but she covers pretty much everything the book covers - uses personal stories and examples and she is so fun and exciting! I would fight to have her as my professor!!

This class is super easy! All you have to do is show up to class (which is a point a day) and take notes. There are 3 tests but they are super easy. There are also 3 papers but I wrote mine in a day and got 100%. There really isn't any extra credit but you shouldn't need it. Would definitely take this class again.

Jodi is very nice her lecture are actually very entertaining she brings her own experiences into lessons and makes us feel like family it is so amazing to see how much Jodi cares about early education and brings in her experiences as a parent herself she is very kind and explains things very well and is also very helpful!

This professor was very kind and clear in her lectures.  There was a lot of time given to discuss amongst ourselves, even though the class was large, which was nice.  Communication was good, and the material was topical and assignments felt well-outfitted.

Jodi is awesome! I had terrible burnout the entire course and struggled keeping up with deadlines, but only 1 point is taken off for late work and it can be submitted before the 2nd to last day of the quarter for 14/15 points. We also had a TA strike, so 1 weekly essay was changed to extra credit that pushed me to a 4.0. I loved this class! Do it!!

analysis.name: Judith Swain
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 19 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: My experience with Judith as a professor seems to have been much different than those listed here. This was a watercolor drawing class that was C/NC, but she was awesome. Although I wasn't the most confident in my skills, she gave great feedback and was very inspirational. Was also super supportive of everyone's work at the end of the quarter!

Learn what you can from the textbook/TA. Swain smiles in public but is apathetic and degrading behind closed doors. I learned most from her poor example. As a person living with a disability, I wish I could fully explain her lack of compassion without being censored here. Proceed with the utmost caution...

She is very plain. I didn't have her for my studio but her lectures were dreadful. Very monotone & doesn't seem excited about anything. She would talk about stuff that wasn't useful at all. I can honestly say that I don't remember a single thing from her lectures. Instead I just read the book & talked to the graduate student ta for information.

Judith is very conceptual with an artsy flair. Sometimes she can get a little bit heady and communication can become clouded. However, I thought she was a valuable resource when utilized. I think her teaching style is more suited for higher level courses instead on ARCH 100.

She isn't helpful unless she likes you. Subjective in grading, is either bipolar or has bad sugar level issues. Does not explain well, uses examples but limited explanation. Likes to let next project be a surprise, so when you realize they all tie in together, you're screwed. Pick a really interesting piece styrofoam you'll b drawing it for weeks.

analysis.name: Jeffrey Ochsner
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 16 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Don't take for an easy class.  While it's 3 credits, it was the hardest class I've ever taken.  That being said, learned SO MUCH and he is an incredible lecturer.

Too many key works compare to ARCH 350 and 351. Have two 10 mins quizzes that quiz about the concepts. A lot of little and unclear information that need to be memorized. I don't really enjoy this class overall.

First time writing on here, never felt the need to before but Jeffrey changed my mind. He is passionate on the topics he speaks about, his passion is so genuine that every lecture I am always so invested. Im not an architect major nor interested in architecture, yet this class was fun! Pay attention and study for assignments, and you will do great!

If you're considering taking this class, you should. Jeffrey is so knowledgable on everything related to Seattle architecture and the class itself is really fascinating. He's also a great guy and pretty funny too. The class isn't easy, but you'll do well if you come to lectures and put forth your best effort. Grading can be tough.

I would try your best to avoid this professor. Grading scale and feedback is based on if he personally likes you and your writing style. Rambling surface-level lectures. Talks down to students and argues with other professors in an incredibly unprofessional manner. Think of the most annoying tenured professor and you have Jeffery Ochsner.

analysis.name: Richard Jacroux

analysis.name: Julian Olden

Lab section is really interesting if you have the credit space.

great professor! took the 5 credit (lecture & lab) was very passionate about lectures, and what he was teaching. included his own stories in lectures, and made it really fun. took this class for my bio credit, and would take again! midterm and final were doable, no outside reading, but weekly quizzes and summary sheets!

Tough grader, lots of trick questions, and not very forgiving if you miss class. Class structure changes each year he teaches the class. Good professor and very enthusiastic about the content. Was very particular about certain things and the weekly summary sheets seemed like a lot of work for the payoff of them. Tests were long, very handwritten and long. Interesting class though

analysis.name: Jonah Ostroff
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 222 reviews — this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: The Prof-Stroff! Jonah is extremely passionate about math and instruction. He's personable and extremely fair. His lectures are very well structured and he provides every resource for you to succeed. The one drawback is that his tests aren't the hardest, and people are very engaged in class, so the averages can be high. Ask about his math songs.

Posts all his notes online unlike other profs but I would still recommend going to class because he can really teach. I loved his nerd jokes. The only problem is that he's such a good prof no one is lost so you can't expect much curve

Lectures were easy to follow along with, may be hard to keep on track with the videos & hw, very test-heavy, tests were challenging but nothing you wouldn't be prepared for if you studied. extremely cool guy! would rec the online ver. great, pre-recorded lectures and minimal hw. you can and should go in-person if you want!! pretty sure he didn't know i was asynch until the final (you take it in different rooms). go to office hours (jonah has 3 office hours a week! honestly really cool of him) and it's basically impossible to fail.

analysis.name: Marjorie Olmstead

review_highlights.comment: Theres no doubt Prof. Olmstead knows her stuff, but it feels like she expects you to know everything she does. She is very organized, in the wrong way. She has an amazing capability to make even the most simple physics concepts confusing, even if they are things you already know. She also loves to throw in completely useless information about the world of complex physics and does lots of useless demos that take a lot of time.

Lectures are dull and unhelpful compared to reading the textbook, professor always flops demonstrations. Labs are unhelpful to concepts taught in lecture yet tested on in exams. She cares more about seeming smart than actually teaching the class and fails in both.

Your better off just watching a youtube video or sticking straight to the textbook, because her lectures don't add anything of substance. What's the point of a class where the professor just reads her slides, no further explanations, no thought processes explained.

analysis.name: Sam Otim

Thick accent, and makes mistakes when coding often. If you miss a participation, you're cooked. If you miss a lab, drop the course to S/NS. 1 midterm, 1 final, some quizzes

There is a group project, final, midterm, and heavy assignments.

I thought CEE 220 was a great class, especially with Eberhard. The class is homework heavy, but manageable. Eberhard seems like he really cares and values teaching and is knowledgeable about the content. Very nice guy in office hours and makes tests very fair if you understand the concepts.

Make sure you sign up for the labs (CSE 190). Do Practice-It problems, probably every day. Do at least a few programming questions/week from past exams/practice exam handouts. He's really cold and when you're in for office hours, it feels like he wants you to leave.  Lectures were okay, pretty basic stuff. Not much to say really; he just gives lectures and leaves. HW was quite long and graded very harshly. Little to no examples were given during class on how to write effective proofs and exams were beyond what he taught in lecture.
